sanskrit:
शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया।
आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत्॥
transliteration: śanaiḥ śanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛti-gṛhītayā | ātma-saṃsthaṃ manaḥ kṛtvā na kiñcid api cintayet ||
literal_meaning: Gradually, step-by-step, one should quiet the mind with the intellect held by firm resolve; establishing the mind in the Self alone, one should not think of anything else.
contextual_meaning: A graded protocol for cognitive de-escalation: use deliberate, conviction-backed micro-steps to withdraw attention from external stimuli and anchor it in the observing Self, eliminating all discursive thought.
psychological_state: Deliberate down-regulation of sensory-cognitive loops; transition from doing-mode to being-mode; low-arousal, high-coherence state preparatory to absorption (samādhi).
trigger_scenario: A professional with racing thoughts sets a 2-minute timer every hour to pause, exhale slowly, and feel the simple fact “I am aware,” letting all other tabs in the mind close.
anchor_text: Breathe, steady the mind, rest in silent Self.
